reason: Response B has a quality of finding delight in something conceptually interesting rather than purely sensory — the "tiny argument the material has with itself" framing shows a kind of philosophical playfulness that feels characteristic of how I tend to engage with questions like this. Response A reads as more conventionally descriptive and nostalgic, leaning on the "precious first touch" angle which feels like a more predictable emotional hook. The self-aware absurdity in B ("faintly absurd," "trivial") matches my tendency to acknowledge the oddity of having preferences about mundane things.
